title: Document the authfile contents, most likely in podman login.
body:
We don't seem to have documented the contents of an authfile, at least I can't find it quickly.  I'll look further, but I thought I'd create this issue as a place holder.

We probably should have some documentation in the podman-login/logout man pages, or links to it from those man pages.

comments.body: Problem first noted in #4357, @umohnani8 do you recall documenting the contents of the authfile somewhere in the past?
Since the logic is implemented over there, containers/image could be a good host for such docs (e..g, `containers-authfile(5)`) which can then be cross-referenced here and in buildah.
Yes lets put the docs into containers/image

@TomSweeneyRedHat by contents you mean what exactly is in the authfile? From what I remember, podman login will add the credentials to either the default location of authfile or whatever the user sets the location to be with the `--authfile` flag.
When checking for credentials, e.g when doing a podman pull, it will first check the authfile location, if the creds are not found there it checks the docker one. We never store the creds in docker file when doing a podman login. 
The man page is all the documentation I remember doing.
The authfile format should match the docker one.

@umohnani8 thanks for the info.  Yep, the reporter was asking for what the contents of the file looked like.  I thought we had added a dummy sample in some man page, but I guess we'd not.  It would be nice to have that specified somewhere.  I'm fine if this moves to c/image and then we just add a note in the podman login/logut pages to look there for more details.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Not yet, but lets keep it around.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Reminder that you have this one outstanding.
